verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is classified as a soluble protein, in contrast to IZUMO1-3 which are transmembrane proteins. Evidence consistently identifies IZUMO4 as an intra-acrosomal protein of sperm that is lost after the acrosome reaction, consistent with a soluble intravesicular compartment rather than a surface-exposed pool. The NCBI summary places it in the nucleus; the Human Protein Atlas notes cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells. No surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, or other evidence demonstrates outer-leaflet accessibility by the protein body. No TM partner tethering or stable surface anchoring has been reported. The protein body is intracellular/intra-acrosomal.
